key_highlights: Stocks hit record highs amid US-Iran ceasefire hopes and resilient economic data, with S&P 500 and Nasdaq closing at records and Dow up 790 points on April 30.
Fed holds rates steady due to Iran war fueling inflation, but strong jobless claims (189k, lowest in decades) and GDP growth lower rate cut odds.
Oil prices soar to 4-year highs but fall back on US-Iran hopes; gas prices surge to $4.39/gallon, biggest one-day jump since Iran ceasefire announced.
Gold notches second weekly loss as US-Iran talks hang in limbo, but Goldman Sachs highlights top gold picks (AngloGold Ashanti, Agnico Eagle, Newmont, Kinross, BHP).
Big Tech earnings test record rally: Apple rallies on Q2 earnings and Q3 guidance, Meta falls on higher AI capex outlook, Microsoft gets BofA reset.
AI investment boosts economic growth, but consumers tap brakes; Cerebras targets up to $4B IPO, lidar stocks (Hesai, Teledyne, Ouster) gain attention.
Private credit fears emerge as Milken looms; Spirit Airlines faces possible shutdown; West Marine readies bankruptcy.
Middle East crisis accelerates Asia's electrification drive; UAE exit from OPEC signals closer alignment with US interests.
Disney slashes stock-based compensation for tech employees; Apple ends net-cash neutral policy; incoming CEO John Ternus makes first public comments.
Retail traders burnt on both sides of April's chip rip; fast-money hedge fund trades roar as Wall Street defies gloom.

sectors.trend: Record highs driven by AI investment and strong earnings from Apple, but Meta falls on higher AI capex outlook; chip stocks volatile with retail traders burnt.
sectors.implications: AI spending remains a key driver, but capex concerns may weigh on sentiment; Apple's supply constraints and Mac mini demand from AI developers highlight hardware tailwinds.

sectors.trend: Oil prices at 4-year highs due to Iran war, but fall back on ceasefire hopes; gas prices surge to $4.39/gallon; Exxon and Chevron earnings fall despite soaring oil prices.
sectors.implications: Geopolitical risk premium remains high; energy stocks may benefit from elevated prices but earnings miss suggests cost pressures; jet fuel costs hurt airlines (Air Canada).

sectors.trend: Gold notches second weekly loss as US-Iran talks hang in limbo; Goldman Sachs highlights top gold picks (AngloGold Ashanti, Agnico Eagle, Newmont, Kinross, BHP) with strong balance sheets and reserve expansions.
sectors.implications: Gold may remain under pressure if ceasefire talks progress, but geopolitical uncertainty and inflation support long-term demand; mining stocks offer value with strong fundamentals.

sectors.trend: Air Canada pulls 2026 outlook on fuel costs; Spirit Airlines faces possible shutdown; Uber adds hotel bookings and vacation rentals to become one-stop travel shop.
sectors.implications: Jet fuel costs are a major headwind; Spirit's potential shutdown could reduce capacity and support pricing for survivors; Uber's expansion into travel could disrupt traditional OTAs.

sectors.trend: Chipotle stock rallied today; Disney slashes stock-based compensation for tech employees; Brooks Apparel resonates with Chinese runners.
sectors.implications: Consumer spending remains mixed; Chipotle's rally suggests strong demand, while Disney's cost-cutting reflects margin pressure; international expansion opportunities in China for brands like Brooks.

sectors.trend: Stryker misses quarterly estimates on muted demand; DENTSPLY SIRONA among best medical stocks under $30; Seaport (psychiatry drugmaker) rises 10% after trading debut.
sectors.implications: Muted demand in medical devices may signal slower hospital spending; psychiatry and specialty pharma IPOs show investor appetite for innovation.

sectors.trend: Hesai Group raised the bar for lidar industry; Teledyne Technologies among best lidar stocks; Aeva targets 100% revenue growth again; Ouster also highlighted.
sectors.implications: Lidar sector gaining traction as autonomous driving and robotics demand grows; multiple players competing, but Hesai's leadership and Teledyne's diversification offer different risk profiles.

sectors.trend: Fresh concerns for private credit as Milken looms; US economic resilience lowers rate cut odds; Axos Financial trades at lofty premium.
sectors.implications: Private credit faces headwinds from higher-for-longer rates and potential defaults; banks like Axos may be overvalued; Fed's steady stance supports net interest margins for some lenders.

key_themes: Geopolitical risk and oil price volatility from Iran conflict
AI investment boom driving tech earnings and capex
Fed's hawkish hold amid sticky inflation and strong labor market
Gold and mining stocks as inflation hedges with strong fundamentals
Lidar and autonomous driving technology gaining momentum
Private credit and financial sector risks from higher rates
Consumer spending divergence: resilient luxury vs. pressured discretionary
Supply chain constraints in tech (Apple Mac mini) and energy (jet fuel)
IPO market revival: Cerebras, Seaport, Pershing Square
Regulatory and political risks: China vehicle ban, FCC review, Columbia credit rating

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Geopolitical escalation in Middle East (Iran conflict)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Diversify into defensive sectors (gold, energy) and maintain cash reserves; monitor ceasefire talks and oil price movements.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Sticky inflation and Fed rate policy uncertainty
risk_assessment.mitigation: Favor short-duration bonds and floating-rate instruments; avoid overvalued growth stocks sensitive to rate changes; consider TIPS for inflation protection.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Oil price surge and jet fuel costs hurting airlines and transportation
risk_assessment.mitigation: Avoid airline stocks with high fuel cost exposure; consider hedging via energy ETFs or oil futures; monitor Air Canada and Spirit for sector weakness.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Private credit and financial sector stress from higher-for-longer rates
risk_assessment.mitigation: Reduce exposure to private credit funds and BDCs; favor well-capitalized banks with strong deposit bases; monitor Milken conference for sentiment.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Tech sector valuation and AI capex overspend
risk_assessment.mitigation: Take profits on high-flying AI stocks; diversify into value and dividend-paying tech; monitor Meta and Microsoft capex guidance for signs of overinvestment.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Consumer spending slowdown and retail bankruptcies
risk_assessment.mitigation: Avoid discretionary retail and travel stocks; focus on essential consumer goods and discount retailers; monitor West Marine and Spirit for broader weakness.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Regulatory and political risks (China vehicle ban, FCC review, Columbia credit rating)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Reduce exposure to Chinese ADRs and auto parts suppliers; monitor bipartisan bill on Chinese vehicles; avoid companies with heavy regulatory exposure.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Supply chain constraints (Apple Mac mini, Thermos recall, chip shortages)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Monitor Apple supply chain for potential revenue impact; avoid companies with single-source dependencies; consider semiconductor equipment stocks as beneficiaries.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy gold mining stocks with strong fundamentals and reserve growth
opportunities.rationale: Gold prices remain elevated despite recent pullback; Goldman Sachs highlights top picks with strong balance sheets and expansion projects (AngloGold Ashanti, Agnico Eagle, Newmont, Kinross, BHP). Inflation and geopolitical uncertainty support long-term gold demand.

opportunities.recommendation: Invest in lidar and autonomous driving technology leaders
opportunities.rationale: Hesai Group raised the bar for the lidar industry; Teledyne Technologies offers diversification; Aeva targets 100% revenue growth. Autonomous driving and robotics demand are accelerating, with multiple catalysts ahead.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy Apple on supply constraints and AI-driven Mac mini demand
opportunities.rationale: Apple rallied on Q2 earnings and Q3 guidance; Mac mini supply constraints from AI and OpenClaw developers indicate strong demand. Incoming CEO John Ternus signals continuity. Apple's end of net-cash neutral policy may allow more investment.

opportunities.recommendation: Consider energy stocks with strong cash flows and dividends
opportunities.rationale: Oil prices at 4-year highs benefit producers; Exxon and Chevron earnings fell but cash flows remain strong. New Canada-US oil pipeline approval supports long-term infrastructure. Big Oil bosses warn of energy cliff, suggesting supply constraints.

opportunities.recommendation: Invest in AI infrastructure and semiconductor equipment
opportunities.rationale: AI investment boosted economic growth; Cerebras targets up to $4B IPO; BofA lifts HPE target on agentic AI shift. Chip stocks volatile but long-term demand for AI chips and data center equipment remains strong.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to airline stocks and increase cash position
defensive_moves.rationale: Air Canada pulls 2026 outlook on fuel costs; Spirit Airlines faces possible shutdown; jet fuel prices surging. The sector faces significant headwinds from oil price volatility and potential demand slowdown.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge against inflation with TIPS and commodity ETFs
defensive_moves.rationale: Fed holds rates steady as Iran war fuels inflation; gas prices hit $4.39/gallon; oil at 4-year highs. Inflation expectations remain elevated, making inflation-protected securities and commodity exposure attractive.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Diversify into dividend ETFs and defensive sectors
defensive_moves.rationale: Market at record highs with geopolitical risks; Schwab U.S. Dividend Equity ETF may offer stability. Defensive sectors like healthcare (Seaport, Envista) and consumer staples provide downside protection.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to private credit and high-yield bonds
defensive_moves.rationale: Fresh concerns for private credit as Milken looms; US economic resilience lowers rate cut odds, keeping borrowing costs high. Default risk may increase in leveraged loans and private credit.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Avoid Chinese ADRs and auto parts suppliers due to regulatory risk
defensive_moves.rationale: Senators introduce bipartisan bill to ban Chinese vehicles and auto parts; FCC head orders review of ABC broadcast licenses. Geopolitical tensions and regulatory crackdowns increase risk for Chinese-linked stocks.

market_outlook.short_term: Markets likely to remain volatile with a positive bias as record highs are tested. Key drivers: US-Iran ceasefire talks (oil price direction), Fed rate path (inflation data), and Big Tech earnings (AI capex). Oil prices could spike further if talks fail, pressuring equities. Gold may stabilize if ceasefire stalls. Consumer spending data (jobless claims at 189k) supports resilience but retail bankruptcies (Spirit, West Marine) signal weakness. The dollar index fell 1.77% in April, supporting emerging assets.
market_outlook.long_term: AI investment boom and economic resilience support a bullish long-term outlook, but risks from geopolitical instability, sticky inflation, and high valuations warrant caution. Gold and mining stocks offer inflation hedging. Lidar and autonomous driving are emerging growth areas. Energy transition (Asia electrification, UAE OPEC exit) creates opportunities. Private credit and high-yield bonds face headwinds from higher-for-longer rates. IPO market revival (Cerebras, Seaport) indicates risk appetite, but retail traders burnt on chip volatility suggests froth.
key_catalysts: US-Iran ceasefire negotiations and oil price direction
Fed rate decision and inflation data (CPI, PCE)
Big Tech earnings and AI capex guidance (Meta, Microsoft, Apple)
Gold price movement and mining sector earnings
Private credit market health and Milken conference outcomes
China regulatory actions and trade policy (vehicle ban)
Consumer spending and retail earnings (Chipotle, Disney)
Energy sector earnings and production decisions (OPEC+ meeting)
Geopolitical developments in Middle East and Asia
watch_list: WTI and Brent crude oil prices
US 10-year Treasury yield and dollar index
Gold spot price and gold mining ETF (GDX)
Weekly jobless claims and nonfarm payrolls
Big Tech earnings (AAPL, MSFT, META, GOOGL, AMZN)
Apple Mac mini supply chain news
Lidar sector earnings (HSAI, TDY, AEVA, OUST)
